I loved lees presentation very engaging and relevant and inspiring.

Overall brilliant event and look forward to next year

column18: Full marks! The content was extremely relevant to marketing in todays landscape. I am at Sony Pictures (Emma) -  so we are in an interesting position where often we are the passion people have, though we also know that aligning with Australians passions is what makes us become a true cultural event. Everything Lee said resonated with how we are thinking about marketing today, and the things we are trying to achieve as a business. It was great to see News has similar thinking and objectives, and understand how they are thinking about this across their ecosystem. Hearing from Edwina and Ben was fantastic - I found it interesting how much they new about the algorithm and its role in obtaining eyeballs on their content - of course we know this to be true, but they both had such a deep understanding and grasp on it, which was interesting to note from the perspective of two legendary editors and how they are thinking about content. Our Brainstorm was great - Tom was an excellent facilitator, it is one of the better ones i have been involved in, I left feeling like everyone understood our business and the ideas generated had the right level of cultural impact, I am looking forward to seeing how News brings these to life. I left feeling like News Australia were a well-oiled machine in terms of their teams working together - from sales, to strategy to editorial, everyone had a cohesive understanding of what they were needing to achieve as a business and clearly work closely together to make it happen. I also left feeling clear about how we can work with News to achieve our business objectives with more scope than I have thought in the past. A very valued session and I'm in awe at how the day felt equally productive and relaxing, not at all draining. Thank you team for an inspiring time.
column19: would love a copy of Lee's presentation as a follow up. Otherwise - no notes!

column18:
Loved the morning presentation and the set up for why passions matter, understanding the audience journey (from casual to obsession) and how fans engage with passions. It was a great session.

The afternoon workshop was a great idea and structure/process, but the key passions identified for clients were fairly generic and category expected e.g. "food and drink" while others were rather obscure e.g. "puzzles and board games" which felt more like an "interest" rather than a passion (that someone might strive to master or obsess over).

As a result, the workshop limited how we might activate relevant passions in a new/interesting/innovative way and did not quite do justice to the untapped "power of passions" and potential of News.

Great presentation on the Power of Passion, highlighting how content plays a pivotal role in connecting audiences, publishers, and brands. It was a privilege to hear from Ben and Edwina as part of the panel.

The workshop was excellent. Tom was outstanding—navigating many strong voices and differing opinions, capturing all perspectives, and shaping them into an actionable framework for the campaign.
column19: The format, people and content were excellent - I wouldn't change a thing!

column18: Interesting and relevant, thank you!

Loved the research of fandoms and how we can tap into the different 'fuel/motivators' and also understanding at what level on fans are at in their journey (obsessed vs casual). I appreciated the different passions that were drawn out for our specific client but would love to understand further if there are certain ways to tap into different fans based on their fuel e.g. are swimmers despite their level of fandom all motivated by community OR is an obsessed swimmer more fuelled by mastery and a casual swimmer is fuelled by community - more out of curiosity than anything but the overall presentations and workshops were fantastic :)
column19: A more direct link between the fandom research and the q&a session e.g. vogue fandoms vs DT fandoms, would have been interesting but both were great on their own.
